job-type.description: Roll 1D6 to determine what kind of job the runners are doing.

meet-location.description: Roll 1D6 to determine where the initial meeting with the employer takes place.

rows.location_type: Warehouse, Loading Dock, Factory, or Other Underused Site
Barrens District or Other Urban Hellhole

macguffins.description: Roll 1D6 to determine the central element of interest (target) to the story.

rows.macguffin: Urban building, rural location or infrastructure object

employer.description: Roll 2D6 and add together to determine who is employing the runners.

twists.description: Roll 1D6 to inject a complication into the run.

rows.twist: A third party is also interested
The target is not what it appears to be (group was lied to)
The job requires a rare piece of equipment
Target has been moved or is being moved
The employer decides to double-cross the runners

physical-traits.description: Roll 1D6 to generate a physically distinctive trait for an NPC.

rows.trait: Dramatically high or low weight and/or height for metatype

personality-traits.description: Roll 1D6 to generate a personality trait for an NPC.

htr-team-response-time.description: How quickly an HTR team responds based on the security level of the location.

rows.example: Downtown Seattle, megacorp HQ, military installation
Mid- to high-level residential, common corporations

tactics-references.description: Page references for security tactics used against intruders.

device-ratings.description: Standard Matrix Device Ratings for common security and infrastructure devices.

cyberware-scanner.description: Thresholds for millimeter wave/cyberware scanners detecting implants and prohibited items, plus situational modifiers.

chemical-detection-modifiers.description: Modifiers to olfactory scanner dice pools for detecting explosives and ammunition.

rows.situation: Every 10 rounds of ammunition
Every grenade
Every 30 grams of (non-plastique) explosive
Every 100 grams of plastique
Item contained in plastic

fencing.description: Thresholds and damage for climbing past various types of fencing. Climbing + Agility (threshold) Test; failure causes listed damage.

license-examples.description: Common license types and the situations in which they are required.

rows.situation: Any item with Availability 'R'
License to practice magic; registered Awakened Individual
Knowledge and use of a single Combat Spell
Registered technomancer; license to use Resonance abilities
Registered professional (doctor, nurse, electrician, private detective, etc.)

fake-sin-details.description: What data is present in a fake SIN at each Rating level.

rows.attributes: Random anybody; age, nationality, and sex may not match; no supporting data
Rough match; sex matches, age and nationality 'pretty close'; no supporting data
Good match; sex, age, and nationality match; supporting data, but obviously fake
Casually plausible; sex, age, and nationality match; supporting data appears valid only on cursory checks
Good fit; all statistics match; valid biometrics for another person (with samples); some supporting data and history
Alternate life; all statistics match; valid biometrics with samples; complete and entirely believable history

sin-verification-details.description: What is checked at each SIN verification Rating level. Use Simple Device Rating x 2 Test, threshold = fake SIN Rating.

rows.what_is_checked: Basic redundancy check on the number and vital statistics
Redundancy check on number and statistics; query for external data attached to SIN
Verify all vital statistics; external data checked for obvious conflicts; biometric must be present
Full verification and consistency check; biometrics tested against sample
All possible verification; multiple biometric samples must match; random supporting data verified externally

public-awareness.description: What level of public recognition a runner has based on their Public Awareness score.

rows.awareness: Unheard of outside the shadow community
Known to those who watch the shadows — conspiracy theorists, specialty law enforcement
Known by those in the know, investigative journalists, law enforcement, some government officials
Household name; sim and trid stars portray the character in movies

lifestyles.description: All lifestyle levels with monthly costs and brief descriptions.

rows.description: Best of everything: ritzy digs, high-tech toys, best food and drink, household staff, maid service, sophisticated drones. Massive mansion, snazzy condo, or penthouse suite in top hotel. Home security is top-of-the-line with well-trained guards, astral security, and quick HTR response times. VIP list at exclusive clubs. High-level executives, government bigshots, Yakuza bigwigs, high-scoring shadowrunners.

Roomy house or condo, good food, technology that makes life easy. Well-to-do lifestyle: mid-level managers, senior Mob bosses. Home is in secure zone or protected by bribes to local police contractor and gang boss. Housekeeping service or tech. Secure zone or solid bribes.

Nice house or condo with lots of comforts. May eat nutrisoy as well as higher-priced natural food, but autocook has full suite of flavor faucets. Ordinary wage-earners or reasonably successful criminals.

Apartment; nobody likely to bother the character as long as they keep the door bolted. Regular meals; power and water available during assigned rationing periods. Security depends on regular payments to local street gang. Factory workers, petty crooks, others just starting out or down on their luck.

Low-grade nutrisoy and yeast. Home is a squatted building, possibly converted to barracks or divided into closet-sized rooms. May share with other squatters. Only thing worse than Squatter is living on the streets.

Character lives on streets, in sewers, steam tunnels, condemned buildings, or whatever temporary flop available. Food wherever found. Bathing is a thing of the past. Character's only security is what she creates herself. Down-and-outers of all stripes.

Special lifestyle that applies only when a character is sick or injured. Confined to a hospital, clinic equipped as a hospital, or a private location with necessary equipment. Characters cannot own this lifestyle — they only pay until they get well or go broke, whichever comes first.

lifestyle-options.description: Optional modifiers to lifestyle cost and mechanical effect. Available for any lifestyle except Streets or Hospitalized.

rows.mechanical_effect: Workshop, garage, office, studio, or large area for specialized work. Skill checks relevant to the setting have their Limit increased by 2 when performed in this space.

Neighborhood surrounded by wall, vigilant security contractors, patrolled by altruistic gang, or near powerful individual. All HTR Response (p.356) and other security response rolls made one level better than the area would normally be.
Particularly difficult to find or in an obscure location. Any Sneaking Skill checks (owner of property excluded) made in the vicinity of the residence are made with a -2 penalty.
Just enough space to live; no room for hobbies, work, or stretching legs. Skill checks tied to Logic have their Limit reduced by 2 (to a minimum of 1) when performed in this space.

Area is particularly dangerous and prone to crime and violence. Security and law enforcement respond only to the most desperate need and will be slower to respond. Any HTR Response (p.356) or similar security response rolls are made as if this area were one security level lower than it actually is.

cash-rewards.description: Cash reward calculation table for runs. Base cost 3,000¥ per player. Apply multiplier (Highest opposing Dice Pool / 4 rounded down + situational modifiers) then cost modifiers.

rows.situation: Highest opposing Dice Pool
Runners were outnumbered three to one in a combat situation
Runners were outnumbered two to one in a combat situation by NPCs with Professional Rating 4 or higher (not cumulative with above)
Runners faced a pack of at least six critters
Runners encountered at least three different spirits (besides watchers) in a single encounter
Runners accomplished the task with impressive speed and/or subtlety
Runners risked public exposure or a raised profile as a natural part of the run
Job brings runners into direct contact with a notably dangerous part or element of Sixth World lore (the Red Samurai, a Mitsuhama Zero Zone, etc.)
Standard run (nothing special, normal work for a runner)
Run will make you a cold-hearted bastard (wetwork, helping corps oppress people, drug running, human trafficking)
Run has good feelings as part of its reward (hooding, helping the little guy, taking some 'dys' out of 'dystopia')

karma-rewards.description: Karma reward calculation table. Award to each player after a run. Add up applicable situations, then apply one Karma Modifier.

rows.situation: Character survived
Group completed all objectives
Group completed some objectives
Overall adventure challenge

security-critter-references.description: Page references for Awakened critters commonly used in security roles.

drop-in-locations.description: Pre-built generic location descriptions with typical security profiles for GM improvisation.

rows.security_level_description: Very light; rarely draws law enforcement
rows.notable_security_elements: Possibly shotgun under the bar; local gang may keep trouble away

rows.security_level_description: Extremely tight; corps don't use these without massive security
rows.tech_level: Top of the line internally; Matrix access extremely limited due to location (underground/underwater)
rows.notable_security_elements: High security; limited Matrix access is itself a security measure

rows.security_level_description: Subtle but effective; trouble is bad for business
rows.tech_level: Top of the line wireless and virtual entertainment
rows.notable_security_elements: Security is subtle; Matrix is top of the line

rows.tech_level: Often basic; could be cutting-edge if run by a genius
rows.notable_security_elements: Maglocks, cameras, alarms; sometimes contracted security
Maglocks on rooms, some cameras, sometimes one live staff member; PanicButton risk from residents

rows.security_level_description: Crisp, professional, well-equipped; no obvious weak point
rows.tech_level: Sophisticated redundant systems; all devices tied to a spider-monitored Host loaded with IC
rows.notable_security_elements: HTR responds promptly; magical security; maglocks with any number of verification methods (keys, cards, biometrics); cameras, sensors, traps, drones; Host with IC and spider

rows.security_level_description: Solid but practical; non-lethal methods preferred; sensitive to equipment needs
rows.tech_level: Top-of-the-line medical technology; good Matrix security
rows.notable_security_elements: Non-lethal methods; weapon checks; suspicious implant checks; unauthorized wireless signal checks; very private clinics may have tighter security

rows.security_level_description: Solid; comfort-focused but not lax; population small so faces are known
rows.notable_security_elements: HTR responds with priority on protecting residents; names/faces of residents known to security personnel

rows.security_level_description: Non-existent except for whatever residents bring
rows.tech_level: Usually absent; rare exceptions (e.g., technomancer occupation)
rows.notable_security_elements: May have holes in walls allowing easy entrance; residents may be hostile (squatters, gangers, Awakened predators, ghouls)

reputation-notoriety-qualities.description: Qualities that modify starting Notoriety at character creation. +1 per negative quality, -1 per positive quality.

notoriety-award-examples.description: Examples of actions the GM may award a point of Notoriety for during play.
examples: Insulting or otherwise pissing off a Mr. Johnson
Exceptional bad luck that leads to disaster

public-awareness-award-examples.description: Examples of actions the GM may award a point of Public Awareness for during play.
examples: Fighting with law enforcement or some other High Threat Response team
Blowing something up in a populated area
Broadcasting or posting videos of your crimes on your Matrix fansite

street-cred.notes: Additional Street Cred may be awarded by the GM for particularly noteworthy accomplishments. Street Cred applies as a positive Limit Modifier to all Social Tests where the character's reputation would be known.

notoriety-starting-value.formula: 0 + (count of negative qualities from notoriety list) - (count of positive qualities from notoriety list)
notoriety-starting-value.notes: Characters cannot earn Notoriety for something they already have a point for.

street-cred-to-notoriety-trade.formula: sacrifice 2 Street Cred → reduce Notoriety by 1

sin-verification.outcome_at_threshold: System reports something 'odd'; operator may investigate further
sin-verification.outcome_exceeds_threshold: System reports SIN as false; fake SIN is burned; may notify authorities immediately

burned-sin.notes: Burned status is per-country/jurisdiction, not global. Data sharing between countries is imperfect and not always instantaneous. A SIN burned in one country may not be burned in another, at the GM's discretion.

run-cash-formula.base_cost_notes: May be increased by 100¥ per net hit on Negotiation Test at start of run.
run-cash-formula.multiplier_part_1_notes: Must be an Active Skill used directly against the players. Use permanent enhancements only — no equipment bonuses, conditional or situational modifiers.
run-cash-formula.multiplier_part_2: Sum of applicable situational modifiers from Cash Rewards table

run-karma-formula.notes: Karma is awarded to each player individually. Apply one modifier that corresponds to the same modifier category used for cash rewards.

lifestyle-missed-payment.mechanic: Each time a payment is missed, roll 1D6. If result > consecutive months missed: landlord lets it slide (payment still owed). If result <= consecutive months missed: character is evicted.
lifestyle-missed-payment.primary-lifestyle-consequence: If primary lifestyle payment is missed: downgrade lifestyle one level. Character may owe back rent to bank, loan company, or loan shark.
lifestyle-missed-payment.non-primary-consequence: Non-primary lifestyle unit is simply lost.

lifestyle-permanent-purchase.selling-with-time: Roll 2D6 * 10% of purchase price (if character has couple months to broker)
lifestyle-permanent-purchase.selling-on-the-run: Also roll 1D6 (must dump fast or through an agent)

team-lifestyle.tenant_of_record_requirement: For Low Lifestyle or higher, one member must be tenant of record — legally responsible for debt if team fails to pay.

street-scum.priority-table-modification: Select once from row B, once from row C, once from row D, and twice from row E; OR twice each from rows C and D and once from row E.

high-life.priority-table-modification: Select twice from row A, once from row B, and twice from row C. No Availability limits on equipment.

military-or-mercenary.priority-table-modification: Attribute Points and Skill Points near standard. Resources tripled or more; Availability limits on military equipment ignored. Alternatively, ignore equipment purchase and assign a pool of available equipment.

docwagon.priority-table-modification: Resources selected from GM-determined limited list or assigned based on role. Otherwise close to standard.

criminal-syndicates.priority-table-modification: Points remain the same as standard. GMs may restrict Availability on some equipment.
